articles.title: CRYPTO INDUSTRY ASKS TO STOP JPMORGAN'S TAX ON DATA ACCESS
articles.summary: Ten fintech and crypto trade groups asked President Trump to stop JPMorgan from charging aggregators for consumer banking data access, calling it a “punitive tax” that could debank millions and slow stablecoin and self-custody wallet adoption. JPMorgan's fees could run ~$300M annually, or 75% of revenue, for Plaid, jeopardizing how users fund exchanges and wallets via services like Plaid and MX. The dispute centers on the CFPB's Rule 1033, which mandates free access to consumer data. Industry leaders, including Kraken co-CEO Arjun Sethi, warn this shift risks re-centralizing data access, arguing crypto offers an alternative.

articles.title: BITMINE DOUBLES ETH HOLDINGS TO $2B, LEADS CORPORATE TREASURY RACE
articles.summary: BitMine Immersion now has more than 566,000 ETH (~$2.04B), making it the largest publicly traded Ethereum treasury holder. Backed by Peter Thiel's Founders Fund and Ark Invest, the firm's aggressive accumulation, aiming to control 5% of total supply, added ~$1B in ETH in just 10 days. This move escalates the corporate ETH arms race, outpacing SharpLink and fueling projections of a $20B institutional wave into Ethereum via treasuries and ETFs.

articles.title: ENDGAME: HOW WE USE ZK TO SECURE MEGAETH
articles.summary: Optimistic rollups hinge on fraud proofs, but today's interactive bisection games are slow, costly, and vulnerable to griefing. MegaETH replaces that with non‑interactive ZK fraud proofs: a challenger submits a single zkVM proof that the proposed state is invalid, $100 only when fraud exists. The chain keeps a 7‑day detection window, yet once challenged, finality is restored in ~1 hour, and dishonest sequencers are slashed, eliminating stall attacks. EigenDA supplies the data needed to reconstruct blocks, ensuring anyone can verify and generate proofs, strengthening an efficient, scalable, onchain security model.

articles.title: BINANCE INTEGRATES CIRCLE'S USYC AS YIELD-BEARING COLLATERAL
articles.summary: Binance has integrated Circle's tokenized money market fund, USYC, as off-exchange collateral for institutional derivatives trading, deepening the partnership between two of crypto's biggest players. USYC, comparable to BlackRock's BUIDL, offers yield exposure to U.S. Treasurys and near-instant convertibility with USDC, making it a capital-efficient bridge between tokenized dollars and real-world assets. The integration, which includes native issuance on BNB Chain, marks a major move for Circle's RWA strategy and comes shortly after its IPO and acquisition of tokenization startup Hashnote.

articles.title: PANTERA BLOCKCHAIN LETTER: JULY 2025
articles.summary: Tokenization has shifted from concept to inevitability this year, with over $24B in RWAs sitting on public chains with ~200 issuers including BlackRock, Franklin Templeton, Siemens, and J.P. Morgan. Liquidity is migrating to programmable rails – Figure has processed $13B onchain, Ondo issued $1B in tokenized Treasuries and is adding equities, Robinhood's Stock Tokens were added on Arbitrum, and Coinbase's Base show fintechs racing to own sequencers and infrastructure. Most assets still grant transferability but not full shareholder rights, setting up a fight over governance and ownership. The tipping point will be when daily onchain equity volume tops $1B or tokenized equity AUM hits $100B. Regulatory clarity, app-specific chains, UX/compliance abstraction, and portable KYC layers are the catalysts for onchain becoming the default venue for capital formation, price discovery, and settlement.

articles.title: MOST DAOS DON'T WORK
articles.summary: Most DAOs falter because they lack clear goals, aligned incentives, and functional hierarchy. Token rewards and airdrops often optimize for short‑term or legal risk offloading rather than sustainable governance. For example, Compound drifted into mere treasury management after costly, low-delivery hires, while Aave retained strong team leadership, implemented revenue sharing/buybacks, and kept execution tight, and Lido's dual governance lets stETH holders delay/veto proposals at 1%/10% thresholds. Uniswap's split between DAO, Foundation, and Labs enabled $145M in UNI spending and triggered a delegate resignation, and Arbitrum's hybrid model funds outside managers as Offchain Labs' Timeboost has already sent $2M+ to the treasury. Emerging “BORGs” mix legal entities with automated, onchain processes, emphasizing the broader point that decentralization should be applied where it fits, not by default.

articles.title: FELIX: DUBIOUS SPECULATION
articles.summary: Felix's weekly points program currently awards ~2.5–2.8k USD per point, translating to an estimated 21-40% APY on the capital deployed, depending on assumptions around total point inflation and airdrop share. Even conservative models (<20% airdrop over 20 weeks) imply ~$11/point and a $120M token distribution, while more realistic scenarios (30% airdrop over 15 weeks) push that to ~$21/point and ~$180M total. Latecomer users farming ~94 points/week could net ~$5.9k–$8.8k in airdrops on a $60k position, before accounting for lending yields or referrals. Smaller portfolios (< $20k) may find the entry cost too high, but larger holders can lock in attractive yields and leverage Felix's broad integration with HyperEVM protocols.

articles.title: LAYER3: THE FINAL PIECE IN WEB3 MASS ADOPTION
articles.summary: Layer3 positions itself as the “Google + Duolingo” of Web3 by solving discovery and onboarding through narrative quests and CUBE NFTs that record onchain actions as portable reputation. Its $L3 token underpins a burn-to-launch model for projects, helping drive $16.5M in revenue split ~40%/60% between partners and users. Case studies like Ondo (92% of $1.04M TVL retained) and Jito (70% assets retained after a month) evidence high-quality engagement. Partnerships such as Robinhood's RWA rollout showcase Layer3 as an onboarding and education partner, while v3 evolves it into an “onchain OS” with a built-in wallet and AI-driven campaign automation. The main risk is incentive gaming, and ensuring CUBEs reflect genuine understanding rather than optimized farming.

articles.title: RIPPLE CO-FOUNDER MOVES $140M IN XRP TO EXCHANGES
articles.summary: Chris Larsen-linked wallets have transferred 50 million XRP (~$161M) since July 17, with ~$140M sent to exchange addresses, according to onchain sleuth ZachXBT.

articles.title: STABLECOIN DIRECTORY LAUNCHES TO MAP GLOBAL ADOPTION
articles.summary: Acctual has launched the Stablecoin Directory, a global hub of 240+ verified businesses that accept stablecoins across sectors like food, fashion, and finance.

articles.title: CELESTIA BUYS OUT POLYCHAIN'S TIA, REASSIGNS TO NEW INVESTORS
articles.summary: The Celestia Foundation has acquired Polychain Capital's remaining 43.45 million TIA for $62.5 million and will reassign the tokens to new investors on a rolling unlock schedule from August 16 to November 14.

articles.title: TOKENIZED RWAS ON ETHEREUM HIT $8 BILLION MILESTONE
articles.summary: The value of tokenized real-world assets on Ethereum has passed $8 billion, driven by institutional adoption from asset managers like BlackRock, Franklin Templeton, and WisdomTree.
